the ambulance membership organization submits a new license application in accordance with Section 31A-6c-201; and
the commissioner approves the application.

If an ambulance membership organization makes a change to any of the following, the ambulance membership organization shall provide the commissioner a notice of the change at least 30 days before the day on which the ambulance membership organization makes the change:
the ambulance membership organization's name;
the ambulance membership organization's address;
the ambulance membership organization's telephone number;
the ambulance membership organization's address for the principal place of business;
the ambulance membership organization's mailing address; or
the ambulance membership organization's website address.

section.secline: Section 7. Section 31A-6c-301 is enacted to read:

subsection.amend: An ambulance membership organization licensed under this chapter shall:

establish and maintain a funded reserve account consisting of unencumbered assets of either cash or cash equivalents, equal to at least 20% of the gross earned fee income the ambulance membership organization receives on all active ambulance membership contracts that the ambulance membership organization sells or renews in this state on or after May 6, 2026;

post a surety bond with one or more surety companies that the commissioner approves in an amount of at least $5,000 for every 100 members of the ambulance membership organization who are residents of this state;
maintain additional securities the commissioner requires by rule in accordance with Title 63G, Chapter 3, Utah Administrative Rulemaking Act; and
pay the costs of collection upon a judgment in favor of a member and attorney fees in a successful action brought by member against the ambulance membership organization.
The reserve account described in Subsection (1)(a) shall be:
maintained in a financial institution that the commissioner approves; and
a separate, auditable account for the ambulance membership organization's ambulance membership contracts in force in this state.

section.secline: Section 8. Section 31A-6c-302 is enacted to read:

catline.bold: 31A-6c-302. Charges authorized -- Required disclosures -- Ambulance membership contract requirements.

subsection.amend: An ambulance membership organization may assess a one-time application processing fee to the ambulance membership organization's members that may not exceed $25.

If an ambulance membership organization cancels an ambulance membership contract for any reason other than nonpayment of charges by a member, the ambulance membership organization shall issue a pro rata refund of all periodic charges and membership fees to the member.

An ambulance membership organization, or a person that sells an ambulance membership contract for an ambulance membership organization, shall disclose each charge and fee for each ambulance membership contract to each prospective member.

An ambulance membership organization shall provide the terms and conditions of an ambulance membership contract to each prospective member before the day on which the prospective member enters into the ambulance membership contract.
An ambulance membership organization shall file a copy of each ambulance membership contract with the commissioner before the ambulance membership contract goes into effect.
An ambulance membership contract described in Subsection (5) shall:
identify the ambulance membership organization, including the ambulance membership organization's:
physical address;
website address; and
toll-free phone number;
conspicuously state:
the total purchase price of the ambulance membership contract, including any membership fees; and
that the ambulance membership contract is not an insurance contract;
state:
the terms under which the ambulance membership contract is to be paid;
any cost sharing requirements;
the services the ambulance membership organization shall provide under the ambulance membership contract, and any limitation, exception, or exclusion;
any term, restriction, or condition that governs the cancellation of the ambulance membership contract by either the member or the ambulance membership organization;

that if the member cancels the ambulance membership contract within 30 days after the day on which the member purchases the ambulance membership contract, the ambulance membership organization shall refund to the member:
any one-time charge the member pays that exceeds $25; and
each periodic charge and membership fee the member pays; and
what constitutes acceptable insurance coverage if eligibility for the ambulance membership contract is conditioned on the member's current and continuing health insurance coverage; and
define "medical necessity," if membership coverage of a transport is conditioned on a finding of medical necessity.

section.secline: Section 9. Section 31A-6c-303 is enacted to read:

subsection.amend: An ambulance membership organization may not knowingly sell, offer for sale, or provide an ambulance membership contract to an individual who is enrolled in the Medicaid program.

If an individual who enters into an ambulance membership contract subsequently enrolls in the Medicaid program during the term of the ambulance membership contract, the individual shall notify the ambulance membership organization of the enrollment within 30 days of the day on which the individual enrolls in the Medicaid program.

If the individual notifies the ambulance membership organization in accordance with Subsection (2)(a), the ambulance membership organization shall provide the individual a prorated refund of any consideration the individual pays for the period from the effective date of the Medicaid program enrollment through the day on which the ambulance membership contract expires.
If the individual does not notify the ambulance membership organization in accordance with Subsection (2)(a):
the individual is not entitled to a prorated refund; and

the ambulance membership organization shall unenroll the individual from the ambulance membership contract within 30 days of the day on which the ambulance membership organization receives notice of the individual's enrollment in the Medicaid program.

section.secline: Section 10. Section 31A-6c-304 is enacted to read:

subsection.amend: Each advertisement, marketing material, brochure, ambulance membership card, presentation, and any other communication of an ambulance membership organization shall be truthful and not misleading in fact or in implication.
An ambulance membership organization advertising or marketing the ambulance membership organization's ambulance membership contract to residents of this state:
shall file each written advertisement and marketing material to the commissioner for review in compliance with this chapter; and
may not:
use language in the ambulance membership organization's advertisements or marketing that could reasonably mislead a person into believing that the ambulance membership contract is insurance;

use language in the ambulance membership organization's advertisement, marketing material, brochure, or presentation in relation to the following that could reasonably mislead an individual into believing that the ambulance membership contract is insurance or has been endorsed by the state or a governmental entity:
the ambulance membership organization's licensure or registration with the department or other state department of insurance; or
the ambulance membership organization's relationship to a governmental entity; or
have a restriction on access to the ambulance membership organization, including a waiting period or notification period.

An ambulance membership organization shall make the following general disclosures in writing, in bold, and in at least 12-point font on the first content page of an advertisement, marketing material, or brochure the ambulance membership organization makes available to prospective members or the public:
the ambulance membership contract is a membership plan and is not insurance coverage; and

the toll-free number and website address where the ambulance membership organization's prospective members may obtain additional information about the services the ambulance membership organization offers.

An ambulance membership organization shall provide the disclosures required by Subsection (3) orally to an individual who makes initial contact with the ambulance membership organization by telephone.

Before a person enters into an ambulance membership contract with an ambulance membership organization, the ambulance membership organization shall mail, give, or, with consent of the person, email to the person a separate document that, in bold and in at least 12-point font, states the following disclosures:
the ambulance membership contract is not insurance coverage;
if eligible and covered under Medicare, the prospective member may consult with a representative of the Medicare program to determine:
the extent of applicable Medicare coverage; and
what the prospective member's payment obligations would be if the prospective member were transported by ambulance;

a detailed list of each one-time and periodic fee the ambulance membership organization charges or will charge to the prospective member to join the ambulance membership organization and continue membership in the ambulance membership organization;
the counties and areas in this state that the ambulance membership organization serves, including any restrictions to specific service areas;

if, in an emergency, the prospective member is outside of the ambulance membership organization's service area, that the prospective member may be responsible for the entirety of the cost of the ambulance membership organization's services; and

if a member cancels the ambulance membership contract before 30 days after the day on which the member purchases the ambulance membership contract, the ambulance membership organization shall refund to the member:
any one-time charges the member pays that exceed $25; and
all periodic charges or fees that the member pays.

section.secline: Section 11. Section 31A-6c-401 is enacted to read:

subsection.amend: The commissioner may suspend or revoke a license issued under this chapter if the commissioner determines that the ambulance membership organization:
fails to operate in accordance with this chapter;

advertises, merchandises, or attempts to merchandise the ambulance membership organization's services in a manner that misrepresents the ambulance membership organization's services or capacity for service;
engages in deceptive, misleading, or unfair practices;
does not fulfill the ambulance membership organization's obligations as an ambulance membership organization; or
would be hazardous to the ambulance membership organization's members if the commissioner permitted the ambulance membership organization's continued operation.
If the commissioner makes a determination under Subsection (1), the commissioner:
shall send a written notice to the ambulance membership organization that states the grounds for suspending or revoking the ambulance membership organization's license; and

may suspend the authority of the ambulance membership organization to enroll new members for a period of time that may not exceed 90 days for each occurrence, if the commissioner sends the ambulance membership organization a written notice of suspension that describes the conditions the ambulance membership organization shall meet before the commissioner will reinstate the ambulance membership organization's authority to enroll new members.
An ambulance membership organization may request a hearing no later than 30 days after the ambulance membership organization receives a notice under Subsection (2)(a).
The commissioner may not reinstate a suspended ambulance membership organization's license unless the ambulance membership organization requests the reinstatement.

The commissioner may not grant a request for reinstatement if the commissioner finds by a preponderance of the evidence that the circumstances that resulted in the suspension or revocation still exist or are likely to recur.
The commissioner shall take each action under this section in accordance with Title 63G, Chapter 4, Administrative Procedures Act.

section.secline: Section 12. Section 31A-6c-402 is enacted to read:

sectionText.amend: A person that violates a provision of this chapter is subject to the penalties described in Section 31A-2-308.
This bill takes effect on January 1, 2027.
